sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The Chat View is the primary interaction surface — a Slack-like group conversation where the customer and agents communicate. It handles both the debate phase (structured round-robin discussion) and general messaging. The UI is built with React 19 components, styled with the deep navy/violet engineering cockpit theme.

sections.title: Layout Architecture
sections.content:
The application uses a four-zone layout:

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     TopBar                            │
├────────────┬──────────────────────┬───────────────────┤
│            │                      │                   │
│  History   │     Chat Pane        │  Intelligence     │
│  Pane      │  (or Dashboard)      │  Pane / Activity  │
│  (sidebar) │                      │  Viewer           │
│            │                      │                   │
├────────────┴──────────────────────┴───────────────────┤
│              Bottom Controls (Composer)               │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

- **HistoryPane** (left): Conversation list sidebar, create/switch/delete conversations.
- **ChatPane** (center): Message timeline or Dashboard view (toggled).
- **IntelligencePane** (right): Agent roster + activity viewer when an agent is selected.
- **BottomControls**: Message composer with @ mentions, run/stop/steer controls.

decisions.title: View toggle in center pane
decisions.decision: Chat and Dashboard are toggled within the center pane via tabs or a view switcher in the TopBar.
decisions.rationale: UI-R001 requires both views. UI-R002 leaves the switching mechanism TBD. A toggle in the center pane keeps the sidebar (conversations) and right pane (agents) persistent across views.
alternatives: Split pane — rejected: too cramped on smaller screens
Separate routes — acceptable but loses context continuity

sections.title: Message Component
sections.content:
Each message in the chat timeline is a `<ChatMessage>` component displaying:

- **Avatar**: Color-coded circle with agent initial or user icon.
- **Header**: Agent display name (or "You"), role badge, timestamp.
- **Body**: Markdown-rendered content using a lightweight MD renderer (e.g., react-markdown or marked).
- **Visual distinction**: Each agent gets a unique accent color derived from a hash of their ID. Messages from the customer use a distinct "you" style.

Message types:
- `chat_message` — standard text message
- `debate_turn` — agent's debate contribution (may include structured options)
- `phase_change` — system event (styled as divider)
- `system_notice` — informational (styled as muted inline)
- `steer` — customer steering input (styled with a highlight border)

decisions.title: Hash-based agent colors
decisions.decision: Agent accent colors are deterministically generated from agent ID using a hash-to-HSL function.
decisions.rationale: UI-R011 requires visually distinct agents. Hash-based colors are automatic, consistent across sessions, and don't require manual assignment.
alternatives: Manual color picker — P2 enhancement, not needed for MVP
Fixed color palette — limited to N agents

decisions.title: react-markdown for rendering
decisions.decision: Use react-markdown with remark-gfm for GitHub-flavored markdown rendering.
decisions.rationale: UI-R012 requires markdown support. react-markdown is well-maintained, supports code blocks, tables, and lists. remark-gfm adds task lists and tables.
alternatives: Custom parser — rejected: reinventing the wheel

sections.title: Composer Component
sections.content:
The message composer is a `<Composer>` component with:

- **Textarea**: Multi-line input. Enter sends, Shift+Enter inserts newline (UI-R022).
- **@ Mention Autocomplete**: Typing `@` triggers a dropdown showing agents in the current conversation, filtered as the user types. Selecting an agent inserts `@AgentName` into the text and sets `target_agent_id` on the message payload (UI-R020).
- **Send button**: Visible alongside the textarea.

Message routing:
- If the text contains `@AgentName` or the user selected a target from the dropdown, the message is **directed** — sent to that specific agent (UI-R020, PRD-003).
- If no @ mention, the message is **broadcast** — all agents see it on their next turn (UI-R021).

The composer also includes the run control buttons (Run Batch / Continue / Stop / Steer) below the textarea, accessible during execution phases.

decisions.title: Inline @ mention detection
decisions.decision: Parse @ mentions from the message text rather than using a separate 'To:' field.
decisions.rationale: Matches the natural Slack-like UX. The user types @rick naturally in their message. The composer detects this and sets the target_agent_id. Falls back to broadcast if no mention.
alternatives: Dropdown 'To' selector — rejected: less natural, adds UI clutter

sections.title: Debate Phase UI
sections.content:
During the debate phase, the chat augments with debate-specific indicators:

- **Phase Banner**: Sticky bar at the top of the chat showing the current phase name and round counter (e.g., "Debate — Round 5/20") (UI-R030).
- **Speaking Indicator**: When an agent is currently generating a response, show a typing indicator with the agent's name and avatar (UI-R030).
- **Clarification Banner**: When an agent @ mentions the customer, a prominent amber banner appears: "Agent X needs your input" with a button to scroll to the question (UI-R031).
- **Customer Controls**: Three buttons visible during debate:
  - **Approve & Advance** — approves the current agreement and moves to next phase (UI-R032)
  - **Steer** — sends a steering message (uses the normal composer)
  - **Decide** — ends the debate and lets the customer pick the direction

All controls respect the gate system — Approve sets gate_status to `approved`, triggering a phase transition.

decisions.title: Gate controls as explicit buttons
decisions.decision: Phase transitions are triggered by explicit UI buttons (Approve, Decide), not by typing commands.
decisions.rationale: UI-R032 requires clear customer controls. Buttons are discoverable and prevent accidental phase changes. They map directly to API calls (gate_approval event).
alternatives: Slash commands (/approve) — acceptable as secondary mechanism but buttons are primary

sections.title: Auto-scroll & History
sections.content:
The chat pane implements smart auto-scrolling (UI-R013):

- **Auto-scroll ON**: When the user is at (or near) the bottom of the chat, new messages auto-scroll into view.
- **Auto-scroll OFF**: When the user scrolls up to read history, auto-scroll disables. A floating "New messages ↓" badge appears at the bottom.
- **Scroll restore**: Clicking the badge scrolls to the latest message and re-enables auto-scroll.

Messages are loaded incrementally — initial load fetches the most recent N messages, scrolling up triggers pagination (`?after=` cursor).

sections.title: Conversation Sidebar
sections.content:
The HistoryPane (left sidebar) shows all conversations:

- **Conversation list**: Sorted by updated_at DESC. Each entry shows title, phase badge, and relative time.
- **Create button**: Opens a dialog for title + project directory path (UI-R070).
- **Active indicator**: The currently active conversation is highlighted.
- **Click to switch**: Selecting a conversation calls the `/conversations/select` endpoint and reloads the chat pane (UI-R071).
- **Context menu**: Right-click for delete option.

Conversation state is fully persisted — closing the browser and returning loads the same conversation with its agents, messages, and phase (UI-R072).

sections.title: Agent Roster
sections.content:
The roster appears in the right pane (IntelligencePane) and shows all agents in the current conversation:

- **Agent card**: Display name, role badge (Analyst/Team Lead/Developer), provider icon (Claude/Codex/Ollama), status dot (green=idle, blue=running, amber=blocked, gray=offline), current task title if assigned (UI-R060).
- **Drag to reorder**: Agents can be reordered via drag-and-drop, which updates turn_order for the debate round-robin.
- **Click to view activity**: Clicking an agent opens their activity viewer in the right pane, replacing the roster (UI-R061). A back button returns to roster.
- **Add agent**: Button opens a form for name, role, provider, model (UI-R062).
- **Remove agent**: Each card has a remove button that detaches the agent from the conversation.

sections.title: Notification System
sections.content:
In-app notifications (UI-R080) are implemented as toast messages:

- **Clarification needed**: Amber toast when an agent @ mentions the customer.
- **Max rounds reached**: Warning toast when debate hits max_rounds.
- **PR ready**: Info toast when a PR needs customer attention.
- **Agent blocked**: Alert toast when an agent is blocked/needs input.

Toasts appear in the top-right corner, auto-dismiss after 8 seconds, and are clickable (navigate to the relevant context). Browser notifications (UI-R081) are a P2 enhancement using the Notifications API.
